slides.title: MoQ Performance and Feedback Requirements for Live Media and Agent Interaction

chat.author: Alan Frindell
chat.text: 1.5x the normal amount of fun

chat.author: Lorenzo Miniero
chat.text: The link is here: https://moq.conf.meetecho.com/ietf126-moq/

chat.author: Lorenzo Miniero
chat.text: You'll need Chrome to use it

chat.author: Lorenzo Miniero
chat.text: It automatically translates all WebRTC feeds (and slides) to MoQ

chat.author: Lorenzo Miniero
chat.text: Web side uses Moqtail, so thanks to Ali and his team for making it available for everyone!

chat.author: Jordi Cenzano
chat.text:
Following the MOQ stream
Great work Lorenzo!!!!

chat.author: Lorenzo Miniero
chat.text: Thanks Jordi :heart:

chat.author: Alan Frindell
chat.text: Are we still bashing the agenda?

chat.author: Jordi Cenzano
chat.text: @lorenzo Feature request for MOQ page: Allow fullscreen, with such small video is difficult to evaluate A/V sync on the videos

chat.author: Lorenzo Miniero
chat.text: Good points, thanks for the feedback!

chat.author: Lorenzo Miniero
chat.text: It's a regular video element, though (fueled by MediaStreamTrackProcessor) so if you right-click it and use Show controls, it should allow you to fullscreen from there

chat.author: Jordi Cenzano
chat.text:
I see!! Thanks
What player code are you using?

chat.author: Lorenzo Miniero
chat.text: The MoQ library in the browser is Moqtail, but I only use it for the protocol: I then handle decoding myself in dedicated workers, via WebCodecs

chat.author: Jordi Cenzano
chat.text: Nice! Thanks for the info

chat.author: Jordi Cenzano
chat.text:
(visually) I see AV sync is OKish :-)
I think that is totally OK for this use case. But for broadcast grade experiences we would probably need better

chat.author: 八谷航太
chat.text: Is the application code available on GitHub or somewhere? I used to write player without MediaStreamTrackProcessor so I might be able to make it work on Firefox too

chat.author: Lorenzo Miniero
chat.text: Oh yes, for sure! There's no AV sync happening at all in the code, right now, it's best effort "decode ASAP", with the audio buffer independent of video rendering. But we use LOC-03 and do send timestamps, so we should have the info needed to address that in the future

chat.author: Lorenzo Miniero
chat.text: Kota: not yet but I plan to document the whole effort in a write-up for a blog post, so I may share the demo code too. I know I could have used canvas, for instance, but MediaStreamTrackProcessor seemed "faster" for prototyping

chat.author: 八谷航太
chat.text:
MediaStreamTrackProcessor seemed "faster" for prototyping
That's for sure and also lighter

chat.author: Lorenzo Miniero
chat.text: One thing worth mentioning is that MoQ media are a bit delayed because I needed a jitter buffer on the WebRTC side, to reconstruct full frames out of separate RTP packets to send via MoQ. So if it seems delayed compared to WebRTC it's only because of that

chat.author: Christian Huitema
chat.text: +1 Alan. We really need to ship the transport draft!

chat.author: Harald Alvestrand
chat.text: Experiencew ith WEBRC/RTCWEB was that "small" features added near (what we thought was) the end of the process proved to be extremely costly in time, and also contained issues that only surfaced down the road, even after spec freeze. (I'm thinking simulcast in particular). So adding features to a base spec is not great for finishing a spec.

chat.author: Alan Frindell
chat.text: I think that is good perspective

chat.author: Alan Frindell
chat.text: Is it clear to applications that might use secure objects that if they choose 33 bit object Ids, that can have poorer security properties?

chat.author: Magnus Westerlund
chat.text: I think to be secure one will have to fail objects that uses to long OIDs.

chat.author: Alan Frindell
chat.text: Do we want to consider making MOQT Object IDs max 32 bits?

chat.author: Victor Vasiliev
chat.text: I definitely have had at least one draft before held for the only one implementation reasons before

chat.author: Slackmoq
chat.text:
kota-yata: PUBLISH_NS + SUBSCRIBE succeeds, but no object flows with the publisher receiving STOP_SENDING from subscriber somehow
[2026-07-20 14:56:20.905] [debug] subscriber sent STOP_SENDING for request 1 (error 0)

chat.author: Alan Frindell
chat.text: Given infinite time, who will implement secure-objects?  Is there someone else who wants to use this?

chat.author: Magnus Westerlund
chat.text: Victor, those requirements are usually these communities having chosen to have them for various reasons.

chat.author: Harald Alvestrand
chat.text: I think Transport and Routing tends to impose the 2-implementation requirement, while Apps (WIT/ART/APP/RAI) traditionally has not.

chat.author: Alan Frindell
chat.text: Victoria - lovely city.  Good for watching sportsgame

chat.author: Alan Frindell
chat.text: How is sampling compatible with the security properties you want?

chat.author: altanai
chat.text: Question : Can the relay be mobile ? if yes then is there a process of doing re-auth when moving between diff regions / timezones ?

chat.author: Christian Huitema
chat.text:
(Meeting in Victoria would indeed be nice.)
On the security model -- when I hear "delegating policies" I understand "unbounded increase in complexity".

chat.author: Alan Frindell
chat.text: +1 it seems new.  But MOQ is also serving use cases not handled by HTTP today.

chat.author: Will Law
chat.text: Pirates will bypass any page auth and target the stream directly.

chat.author: Victor Vasiliev
chat.text: A lot of existing auth delegation schemes bind to client's IP address

chat.author: Rohan Mahy
chat.text:
bullet 5, what does this mean?
"We do need a spelling for ..."

chat.author: Magnus Westerlund
chat.text: Working group lingo för an actual text proposal.

chat.author: Magnus Westerlund
chat.text: Or at least detailed proposal

chat.author: altanai
chat.text:
9.4.  Subscriber Interactions
.... Relays MUST ensure subscribers are authorized to access
   the content associated with the Track..... 
This should probably mention cases of re-authorization too,  on part of Subscribers, as was just discussed

chat.author: Michal Hošna
chat.text:
+1 for Alan. 
Auth in MOQT is, currently, hop-by-hop and cannot really be end-to-end.

chat.author: Alan Frindell
chat.text: Since HTTP doesn't really have this bypassed aggregation auth, does RTP?

chat.author: Alan Frindell
chat.text: Could you do this with AUTH_CHALLENGE / RESPONSE as a param on the _OK and REQUEST_UPDATE?

chat.author: Victor Vasiliev
chat.text: I am getting GSSAPI vibes from this diagram

chat.author: Alan Frindell
chat.text: yikes!  Trigger warning required

chat.author: Jonathan Lennox
chat.text: RTP doesn't really have any auth at all; it's all upstream of the RTP being negotiated.  In WebRTC it would be in the web app before you get the offer/answer.

chat.author: Christian Huitema
chat.text: I remember the old MBone model. Just encrypt the media. Authorization boils down to getting the key...

chat.author: Alan Frindell
chat.text: Can we make that model work here to avoid adding transport complexity?

chat.author: Steven Riedl
chat.text: my thoughts exactly, the app needs to handle the auth for geo and a lot of other subscriber reasons, then issue a short TTL bearer token for the relay, I don't see the relay ever being involved with that. Possibly for some other use cases, but not mine.

chat.author: Magnus Westerlund
chat.text: When it comes to RTP there are more than one security model, please see https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/rfc7201/ but that is mostly a question have you gotten the access to the key or can prove your identitiy during key-management.

chat.author: Jonathan Lennox
chat.text: There needs to be some way that the relay recognizes which bearer tokens are legit but I'm not sure that needs to be standardized vs. contractual

chat.author: Jonathan Lennox
chat.text: And yes, having the media be encrypted and having the key distribution out-of-band solves a lot of the problems

chat.author: Alan Frindell
chat.text: I still don't understand why you would want statistical validation

chat.author: Colin Perkins
chat.text: RTP is at a different layer - it relies on a higher layer protocol to do the auth, and the higher layer has to worry about the issues being discussed here.

chat.author: Alan Frindell
chat.text: Is there other work we can look to?

chat.author: Ted Hardie
chat.text: @Alan people have gotten used to stochastic responses to deterministic questions?

chat.author: Jonathan Lennox
chat.text: Alan: there's WebRTC which is just the web model, and there's SIP whose security model should not be the model for anything.

chat.author: Ted Hardie
chat.text: Victoria , BC the city on Vancouver Island or the Australian state in which Melbourne is located?

chat.author: Christian Huitema
chat.text: The classic complexity with relying on encryption is that a secret shared with 1 million subscribers is not much of a secret. So you end up having at least part of the stream encrypted with a per client key.

chat.author: Jonathan Lennox
chat.text: I think the slide showed Victoria, WA

chat.author: Ted Hardie
chat.text: @Jonathan Thanks.

chat.author: Christian Huitema
chat.text: Nice. I could easily sail my boat there...

chat.author: Alan Frindell
chat.text: Boat party!

chat.author: Christian Huitema
chat.text: 28ft sail boat, not a megayacht!

chat.author: Ted Hardie
chat.text: Seperate document will make it easier to close MoQT

chat.author: Colin Perkins
chat.text: And easier to revise in the light of experience

chat.author: altanai
chat.text: I want to volunteer to coauthor the draft for diagnostic data from relay

chat.author: Ian Swett
chat.text: Thanks @altanai

chat.author: Mike English
chat.text:
Alan Frindell said:

How is sampling compatible with the security properties you want?

That was one of the challenges that became more apparent as @Thibault Meunier and I worked through a possible implementation plan yesterday. Protecting against malicious actors ends up requiring the relay to carry some fairly complex state associated with each subscriber within the aggregation set.

chat.author: Suhas Nandakumar
chat.text: we do have to maintain per subscriber state anyways right .. the sampling is picking xth of Y susbcriber to pass on the info

chat.author: Martin Duke
chat.text: Maybe I'm naive but it seems like requests and object delivery can result in stream reset,

chat.author: Martin Duke
chat.text: and those have an error code

chat.author: Ted Hardie
chat.text: But "what are the next steps" may very well not be carried in the error message, just something the receiver of the error knows based on the error it received.

chat.author: Ted Hardie
chat.text: If less is more, is null infinite?

chat.author: Mike English
chat.text:
Suhas Nandakumar said:

we do have to maintain per subscriber state anyways right .. the sampling is picking xth of Y susbcriber to pass on the info

To get the properties you want, you can't just do that naively. Or an attacker can just rapidly create new sessions to skip past the ones that get checked... (etc. etc.)

chat.author: Colin Perkins
chat.text: What are the possible next steps needs to clear. That doesn't mean it has to be in the error message, but the precise error may affect it.

chat.author: Ian Swett
chat.text: To echo Cullen's point, silent timeouts are terrible.
